selection_reason: BSR is the strongest evidence-aligned synthesis across the six perspectives: it directly responds to the pivot diagnosis that the repair object is wrong, keeps compute honest, and preserves a clean sham-control test against RAND-84.

notes: cand_mgcd and cand_dsg failed sham-control separation on the audited screening slice
raw entropy still appears informative, but current evidence supports routing/diagnosis more than direct controller claims
a debate ledger is now materialized at current/idea/debate/summary.md to keep objection-resolution traceability explicit

runtime_contract.backend_policy: prefer flash_attention_2 plus torch.compile, otherwise record a matched fallback for every candidate and control

candidates.title: Boundary-Stable Repair
candidates.summary: Diagnose uncertainty islands, aggregate them into spans, protect stable boundaries, and perform the minimum local revision needed to test whether the repair object—not a richer controller—is the missing piece.
hypotheses: Local span/boundary repair can beat RAND-84 under matched compute
Any gain should come from harm containment rather than wider rewrites
candidates.pilot_focus: Run BSR against RAND-84 and CARD-84 on GSM8K audited slices first, then extend to MBPP with syntax-guard only if the GSM8K gate passes.

candidates.title: Entropy-Stable Parallel Decoding
candidates.summary: Use entropy as a routing and stopping signal to shrink the active frontier and reallocate compute, rather than using entropy as a direct semantic controller.
hypotheses: Entropy is more useful for compute routing than for direct revision decisions
A routing-first method can deliver meaningful speed gains without collapsing against sham control
candidates.pilot_focus: Measure matched-wall-clock speedups, active-frontier shrinkage, and quality retention against RAND-84 and CARD-84 on GSM8K.

candidates.title: Uplift-Gated Revision
candidates.summary: Add a very cheap benefit estimator on top of local uncertainty islands so revision is attempted only when the predicted net gain is positive.
hypotheses: The observer-to-controller gap is partly a missing benefit-estimation problem
Benefit gating can improve the repair/harm ratio over plain BSR
candidates.pilot_focus: Only run after BSR or ESPD shows early signal; compare repair/harm ratio and touched-island count against plain BSR.

archived_negative_controls.title: MGCD: Memory-Gated Consensus Diffusion
archived_negative_controls.reason: Failed sham-control separation on both audited slices and should no longer consume front-runner narrative budget.

archived_negative_controls.title: DSG: Drift-Span Gating
archived_negative_controls.reason: Did not separate from RAND-84 on the audited slice and now serves only as a lighter negative control.
